turns.name: SAMUEL A. ALITO, JR.
turns.text: The second case is Bristol-Myers Squibb Company versus The Superior Court of California. In this case, a group of plaintiffs sued Bristol-Myers Squibb in California state court claiming that they were injured by one of the company's drugs. Most of the plaintiffs are not California residents and they did not allege that they obtained the drug from a California source, that they were injured in California, or that they were treated for their injuries in California. Bristol-Myers Squibb moved to dismiss these claims for lack of personal jurisdiction, but the California Supreme Court held that the state courts had specific jurisdiction based on a sliding scale test. Under our precedents, specific jurisdiction requires what we have described as an affiliation between the forum and the underlying controversy such as an activity or an occurrence that takes place in the forum state. This test is not met here. We therefore reserve the judgment of the Court of Appeals and we remand the case for further proceedings consistent with this opinion. Justice Sotomayor – Excuse me. We reverse the judgment of the California Supreme Court and we remand the case for further proceedings not inconsistent with this opinion. Justice Sotomayor has filed a dissenting opinion.
